items.title: Introducing the OpenAI Economic Research Exchange
items.summary: OpenAI launches the Economic Research Exchange to study AI’s impact on jobs, productivity, and the economy. Applications are now open for selected research projects.

items.title: Everything Apple Announced at WWDC 2026
items.summary: Updates include a new souped-up Siri, lots of iOS enhancements, and some inkling on how an AI partnership with Google has come to power Apple’s products.

items.title: Apple announces Siri AI and its next generation of Apple Intelligence
items.summary: Two years after first revealing its plans for Apple Intelligence and a smarter Siri that never fully materialized, at WWDC, Apple just revealed a new set of AI features and a smarter, more personalized Siri. Apple calls Siri AI an "entirely new version of Siri" and says it's both more conversational

items.title: Apple’s iPhone Camera App Is Getting an AI Upgrade in iOS 27
items.summary: Siri is now directly embedded into the camera app, and there are more artificial intelligence tools in the Photos app to alter your images.

items.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Ready to Get Personal
items.summary: From a stand-alone app to a Google Gemini partnership, here’s everything you need to know from WWDC 2026 about Apple’s upcoming overhaul of Siri.

items.title: Amazon is launching AI-generated custom merch
items.summary: Amazon is expanding its print-on-demand features to AI-generated designs created using Alexa for Shopping for products like T-shirts, water bottles, and hoodies. items.title: The UK Is Betting on a Billion-Dollar AI Supercomputer to Kick Its Addiction to US Tech
items.summary: The British government thinks a state-backed infrastructure initiative will help supercharge homegrown chip startups.

items.title: Meta Deletes Face-Recognition System From Its Smart Glasses App After WIRED Report
items.summary: The code WIRED identified is gone from the latest version of Meta AI, the companion app for the company’s smart glasses. Meta won’t say why or whether it’s coming back.

items.summary: Google is rolling out "across the board" updates to NotebookLM. items.title: Watch Duty Is Adding Flood Alerts to Its Wildfire App
items.summary: The popular wildfire tracking app is adding flood monitoring to its platform. It’s the first new disaster alert on the service, with many more to come.

items.title: MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed: 1T model with 1000 tokens per second
items.summary: MiMo, in collaboration with TileRT, releases the UltraSpeed mode of Xiaomi MiMo-V2.5-Pro — breaking 1000 tokens/s generation speed on a 1T-parameter model for the first time on commodity GPUs through extreme model-system codesign.

items.title: Lenovo IdeaPad Slim 5x Review: The Best Laptop Under $1,000
items.summary: Lenovo's IdeaPad Slim 5x just might be the best laptop you can buy for $850. It's hard to find any major flaws, which is basically unheard of in a laptop at this price.

items.summary: Decision trees and diffusion models are ostensibly disparate model classes, one discrete and hierarchical, the other continuous and dynamic. items.title: xAI is looking more like a datacentre REIT than a frontier lab
items.summary: xAI is renting huge amounts of GPU capacity to Anthropic and Google. Financial engineering ahead of the SpaceX IPO, a real compute shortage, or a genuine datacentre advantage? Probably all three.

items.summary: See how Wasmer used Codex with GPT-5.5 to build a Node.js runtime for the edge, accelerating development 10x to 20x and shipping in weeks instead of months.
